vulnerabilities.title: [axios] axios: Axios DoS via lack of data size check
vulnerabilities.description: Axios is a promise based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js. When Axios starting in version 0.28.0 and prior to versions 0.30.2 and 1.12.0 runs on Node.js and is given a URL with the `data:` scheme, it does not perform HTTP. Instead, its Node http adapter decodes the entire payload into memory (`Buffer`/`Blob`) and returns a synthetic 200 response. This path ignores `maxContentLength` / `maxBodyLength` (which only protect HTTP responses), so an attacker can supply a very large `data:` URI and cause the process to allocate unbounded memory and crash (DoS), even if the caller requested `responseType: 'stream'`. Versions 0.30.2 and 1.12.0 contain a patch for the issue.

exploitability.explanation: Base severity (HIGH): 7.5 | Category risk bonus (Dependency Vulnerability (CVE)): +0.4 | Confidence modifier (HIGH): ×1.00 → Score: 7.9 | Correlation boost +1.5 (xss_vulnerable_sanitizer)

correlation.correlation_reason: XSS finding combined with a vulnerable HTML sanitizer library (CVE dependency)

business_impact.breach_example: Equifax (2017) — Unpatched Apache Struts CVE exposed 143M SSNs. Total cost: ~$700M.
business_impact.potential_impact: Depends on CVE — may include RCE, data exfiltration, service disruption.
business_impact.exploit_scenario: An attacker exploits the weakness at in `reviewpannel-frontend/package-lock.json` to gain unauthorized access, exfiltrate data, or disrupt service depending on application context.

ai_explanation.evidence_from_code: CVE-2025-58754, Axios versions prior to 0.30.2 and 1.12.0
attacker_realistic_path: An attacker crafts a large 'data:' URI and sends it to the application.
Axios, running on Node.js, decodes the entire payload into memory, ignoring maxContentLength/maxBodyLength limits.
The process allocates unbounded memory, leading to a denial-of-service (DoS) crash.
ai_explanation.technical_impact: Uncontrolled memory allocation can cause the Node.js process to crash, leading to a denial-of-service (DoS) condition.
ai_explanation.project_specific_risk: This vulnerability can be exploited in the reviewpannel-frontend project, potentially causing the application to become unavailable.

vulnerabilities.title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: The login endpoint in rolesRoutes.js has no rate limiting, enabling brute-force and credential stuffing attacks.

exploitability.explanation: Base severity (HIGH): 7.5 | Category risk bonus (Missing Rate Limiting): +0.3 | Confidence modifier (MEDIUM): ×0.85 | Context adjustments: is_public_endpoint(+0.8), automated_exploit(+0.9) → Score: 8.07

business_impact.breach_example: Multiple fintech apps — brute-force attacks on PIN endpoints due to missing rate limits.
business_impact.potential_impact: Brute-force credential attacks, account takeover, API abuse, DoS.
business_impact.exploit_scenario: An attacker exploits the weakness at in `reviewpanel-backend/src/routes/admin/rolesRoutes.js` to gain unauthorized access, exfiltrate data, or disrupt service depending on application context.

ai_explanation.code_behavior_summary: The provided code snippet shows a POST endpoint for login without any rate limiting mechanism.
ai_explanation.validated_root_cause: The absence of rate limiting on the login endpoint allows an attacker to perform brute-force attacks.
ai_explanation.evidence_from_code: router.post("/login"
attacker_realistic_path: An attacker sends a large number of login requests with different credentials.
The server processes each request without any delay or restriction.
The attacker can eventually guess or crack the credentials.
ai_explanation.technical_impact: Unlimited login attempts can lead to account compromise and unauthorized access.
ai_explanation.project_specific_risk: In this project, an attacker can gain access to the admin panel and perform malicious actions.
ai_explanation.clear_student_explanation: The provided code snippet shows a POST endpoint for login without any rate limiting mechanism.
step_by_step_attack_simulation: An attacker sends a large number of login requests with different credentials.
The server processes each request without any delay or restriction.
The attacker can eventually guess or crack the credentials.
secure_fix_steps: Implement rate limiting on the login endpoint to restrict the number of attempts within a time window.
ai_explanation.secure_code_example: Use a rate limiting middleware like express-rate-limit and configure it to limit attempts, e.g., 5 attempts per minute.
defense_in_depth: Implement IP blocking for repeated failed login attempts.
Use a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block brute-force attacks.

The endpoint processes each request without limiting the number of attempts.

ai_explanation.secure_fix: Implement rate limiting on the login endpoint to restrict the number of attempts within a time window.
ai_explanation.code_patch_example: Use a rate limiting middleware such as express-rate-limit to limit login attempts, e.g., `app.use(rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 100, delayMs: 0 }));`

vulnerabilities.title: [aws-sdk] JavaScript SDK v2 users should add validation to the region parameter value in or migrate to v3
vulnerabilities.description:
CVSSv3.1 Rating: 3.7 (LOW)

Summary
This notification is related to the use of specific values for the region input field when calling AWS services. An actor with access to the environment in which the SDK is used could set the region input field to an invalid value. Per the AWS shared responsibility model, customer applications should protect instances appropriately, or implement proper input sanitization checks. The AWS SDK for JavaScript v2 reached end-of-support on September 8, 2025, but a defense-in-depth enhancement has been implemented in AWS SDK for JavaScript v3. Please migrate to that version.

Impact
Customer applications could be configured to improperly route AWS API calls to non-existent or non-AWS hosts. While the SDK itself is functioning as designed, we recommend customers migrate to AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 for continued support and enhanced security features.

Impacted versions: All versions of AWS SDK for JavaScript v2

Patches
No security patch is required, this is an informational advisory.

Workarounds
- Implement proper input sanitization in your application code
- Migrate to AWS SDK for JavaScript v3
- Follow AWS security best practices for SDK configuration

References
Contact AWS Security via the vulnerability reporting page or email [aws-security@amazon.com](mailto:aws-security@amazon.com).

Acknowledgement
AWS Security thanks Guy Arazi for bringing these customer security considerations to our attention through the coordinated disclosure process.

vulnerabilities.title: html.security.audit.missing-integrity.missing-integrity
vulnerabilities.description: This tag is missing an 'integrity' subresource integrity attribute. The 'integrity' attribute allows for the browser to verify that externally hosted files (for example from a CDN) are delivered without unexpected manipulation. Without this attribute, if an attacker can modify the externally hosted resource, this could lead to XSS and other types of attacks. To prevent this, include the base64-encoded cryptographic hash of the resource (file) you’re telling the browser to fetch in the 'integrity' attribute for all externally hosted files.
vulnerabilities.cwe_id: CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check
vulnerabilities.owasp_id: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures; A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

vulnerabilities.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-regexp.detect-non-literal-regexp
vulnerabilities.description: RegExp() called with a `leaderEnrollment` function argument, this might allow an attacker to cause a Regular Expression Denial-of-Service (ReDoS) within your application as RegExP blocks the main thread. For this reason, it is recommended to use hardcoded regexes instead. If your regex is run on user-controlled input, consider performing input validation or use a regex checking/sanitization library such as https://www.npmjs.com/package/recheck to verify that the regex does not appear vulnerable to ReDoS.

correlation_summary.reason: XSS finding combined with a vulnerable HTML sanitizer library (CVE dependency)

executive_summary.overall_security_posture: This project has a moderate to high risk security posture, with a significant number of vulnerabilities across various categories.
executive_summary.biggest_risk_area: Dependency Vulnerability (CVE) is the most dangerous here, as it can allow attackers to exploit known weaknesses in libraries and frameworks, potentially leading to severe consequences.
executive_summary.what_the_student_should_fix_first: Focus on addressing the 28 high-severity vulnerabilities, particularly those related to Broken Access Control and Missing Rate Limiting, as they can be easily exploited by attackers.
learning_recommendations: Secure coding practices for dependency management
Access control and rate limiting techniques
executive_summary.encouraging_note: Don't be discouraged by the number of vulnerabilities - it's a great opportunity to learn and improve! By addressing these issues, you'll not only secure your project but also develop valuable skills in secure coding.
